# RetiredHomeLoans

> RetiredHomeLoans (retiredhomeloans.com) helps retired U.S. homebuyers purchase a home — downsizing, relocating near family, or adding a second home — documented on savings and assets instead of a paycheck. It matches retirees with licensed mortgage specialists who close asset-depletion purchase loans, and explains buy-before-you-sell strategies. RetiredHomeLoans is an AdaptLend service (adaptlend.com) and is not a lender.

## Key facts

- Retirees can buy a home with no job: asset depletion programs divide eligible savings/investments over the loan term to create qualifying monthly income (e.g., $750,000 ÷ 240 months ≈ $3,125/month), on top of Social Security and pension income.
- Social Security often counts at up to 125% of the check ("grossing up") because it isn't fully taxed.
- Lenders may not deny a mortgage or shorten its term based on age (Equal Credit Opportunity Act) — a 30-year mortgage at 75 is legal and common.
- Asset-based qualification often lets retirees buy the new home before selling the old one — no double move, no deadline-pressured sale.
- Asset-based purchase programs commonly want 20–30% down; retirees who qualify conventionally on Social Security/pension income can put down less.
- Filling out the RetiredHomeLoans form does not affect credit; a credit check only happens if the borrower moves forward with a specialist.

## How it works

1. Answer a few plain-English questions (about two minutes, no credit check, no login).
2. RetiredHomeLoans matches the borrower with a licensed specialist who closes retiree purchases regularly.
3. The specialist contacts the borrower within one business day with real numbers in writing.

- [Refinancing in retirement](https://retiredrefi.com/)

- [HELOC vs. cash-out refinance guide](https://lumolend.com/guides/heloc-vs-cash-out-refi/) — LumoLend

- [LumoLend](https://lumolend.com/) — price purchase, refinance, and HELOC scenarios with live indicative rates; free calculators and 40+ mortgage guides
